behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both apply: a common real failure is the CLI invoking git without rename detection (or with wrong flags), which yields add+delete instead of rename. A unit/integration test with mocked git output can pass while this real subprocess/git interaction fails. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior text requires true rename classification from the repository state, which is produced by real git diff semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert the report shows exactly one rename and zero paired add/delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: binary handling failures often occur at real git/subprocess output boundaries (encoding, patch format, exit behavior). MOCK-HIDE applies because string fixtures may not reproduce real binary diff behavior. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior explicitly depends on binary diffs produced by git, which is OS/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, commit and then modify/add a binary file, invoke the CLI as a real command, and assert process exits successfully and binary file count/category is present.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: no-commit repos trigger real git HEAD/revision edge-case errors and exit codes. A mock can hide actual subprocess failure modes. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but the behavior is specifically about first-commit repository state as interpreted by real git commands.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert success exit code with an empty structured report (not an exception/error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual git history semantics; failures can come from command selection, revision range handling, and parser/aggregator interaction. MOCK-MISS and MOCK-HIDE apply because synthetic fixtures may not reflect true history behavior. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior text ties correctness to the real repository history, requiring real git execution.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a scripted sequence of commits containing add/delete/rename/modify events, run the CLI, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from the known commit script.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: staged vs unstaged is git index/working-tree behavior exposed through real git commands. In-process fakes often miss index semantics, so MOCK-HIDE applies. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior explicitly compares real staged/unstaged repository states and CLI output.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, prepare equivalent change sets once staged and once unstaged, run the CLI for both scenarios, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured output.
